name: SK hynix Inc.
window: Q1 FY26 print + intra-quarter · Apr 23 → Jun 23, 2026

events.text: Jefferies (Jeff Kim/KBI) argues Korea could out-earn Nvidia in 2027: HBM4 ~35% more expensive than HBM3E and Hynix HBM margins flat (not -20% as feared), with NVIDIA/hyperscalers absorbing the increase. The rally so far has been ASP-led and now needs to turn into unit growth in 2027.

The TSMC ecosystem supplies the logic base die beneath SK Hynix's HBM (Samsung joining the alliance); the expert described Jensen Huang as the supply chain's 'firefighter,' personally chasing Hynix capacity, corroborating Hynix as the primary HBM source on Rubin.

MS: news that Hynix is cutting HBM4 volume/share 'doesn't matter for EPS' — it is better not to have HBM because commodity DRAM returns are higher; but HBM matters for the multiple (DRAM ~4-5x peak earnings vs HBM ~10x). Lower HBM share is EPS-neutral-to-positive but multiple-negative. NAND seen as a more attractive industry than DRAM.

Q1 2026: revenue KRW 52.6tn (+60% QoQ, +198% YoY), operating profit KRW 37.6tn (≈doubled QoQ), OP margin 72% (+13pp QoQ), net income KRW 40.3tn. Net cash KRW 35tn. AI demand drove 'significant price increases in DRAM and NAND'; HBM4 'far exceeding supply capacity.' NAND -10% QoQ off a high base.

JPM: the thesis that Samsung should 'catch up' to Hynix post-strike 'did not work'; Hynix's outperformance is tied to single-stock ETFs + Hynix-centric passive indexing, plus the confirmed US listing/ADR catalyst and HBM4 progress; retail is positioned in Hynix.

GS: cycle longevity is underestimated — Hynix at 5-6x earnings means the market disbelieves the durability; GS's 9x target is 'conservative.' Even a bear case of DRAM -30%/yr in 2027-28 leaves DRAM GM ~40%, supporting the earnings multiple.
Ex-VP of procurement at Micron: HBM becomes a commodity once 2-3 suppliers are qualified; 'your $900 stock goes back to $50' once capacity normalizes — a risk within CY26/early CY27.

JEF calls SK Hynix's ~20% pullback a buying opportunity; the KBI note argues Korean memory can out-earn the hyperscalers. DRAM ASP +50-60% QoQ in 2Q26, +20% QoQ in 3Q26; NAND +75-100% QoQ in 2Q. @rwang07: 'SK Hynix is the next $3 trillion company.'

SK Hynix exits 2026 as #2 in DRAM at ~610K wafers/month (vs Samsung ~740K) but reaches 710K by end-2027, narrowing the gap. It remains dominant in HBM with the largest capacity (~200K end-2026 vs Samsung 180K, Micron 100K). Yongin FAB1 ready end-2027 but output only in 2028; FAB2 just announced. Risk: Samsung could overtake as #1 in HBM in 2027. Anthropic pursuing a deal (DRAM/eSSD 2027, HBM 2028).
HBM supply for 2027 revised down (60→50bn Gb-eq) as conventional DRAM becomes more profitable; LTAs viewed as call options; wafer shortage.
